body.title: Granny shim: self-heal when tickets in queue but daemon dead
body.result: watchdog thread in GrannyShim.start(): stale PID + sprint tickets → kill session + restart daemon; 12 tests
body.intention: I intend that Granny's shim restarts the daemon automatically when sprint tickets exist in the queue but the daemon is not running, so CC never has to manually restart Granny.
body.description:
Granny was dead (daemon killed 2026-06-04, session stale) with sprint tickets in queue. The session existed but the process was gone. CC had to manually restart her — that's wrong. Granny's shim should detect: daemon PID not running + sprint tickets exist → auto-restart the daemon. This is the same self-heal pattern BaseDevice uses for lifecycle management.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/shim.py (or wherever Granny's shim lifecycle is defined), devices/granny/daemon.py

**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim lifecycle pattern (start/stop/self_test); external state principle (shim must not hold restart state in memory)

**Scope boundary:** In scope: shim detects dead daemon + non-empty sprint queue → restart. Out of scope: changing dispatch logic, changing queue format, auto-starting Granny from scratch on boot (that's a separate concern).

**Completion criteria:** Kill Granny daemon process while sprint tickets exist; within 2 poll cycles the daemon restarts automatically and dispatches the ticket; no manual intervention required.
body.description_addendum: Granny restart command (from feedback_granny_restart memory): `tmux kill-session -t granny && ./granny --no-attach`
